page.content: In Table 1, the Home-delivery group (n = 197) shows that 188 individuals (95.9%) reported "Only myself" as the delivery method, compared to 119 individuals (79.9%) in the Facility-delivery group (n = 157), with a statistically significant difference indicated by p < 0.001a. For the Home-delivery group, 6 (3.1%) indicated "Mainly husband," whereas 19 (12.8%) selected this option in the Facility-delivery group. The "Mainly others" category includes 2 (1.0%) from the Home-delivery group and 11 (7.4%) from the Facility-delivery group. There was 1 individual in the Home-delivery group and 8 individuals in the Facility-delivery group classified as "Unknown." In Table 2, the Variable header lists several metrics, with the Number of samples (n) comprising 65 in the Control group, 66 in All Patients, 22 in Early, 31 in Late, and 13 in Recurrent. The GPX3 concentration (ng/ml) Median values are 27.8 for Control, 22.4 for All Patients, 28.1 for Early, 18.5 for Late, and 14.7 for Recurrent. The Maximum values are 53.7 (Control), 49.5 (All Patients), 49.5 (Early), 44.6 (Late), and 48.9 (Recurrent), while Minimum values are 8.7, 4.5, 14.4, 4.5, and 7.7 respectively. Statistical Analysis reveals p-values for comparisons: Vs. Controls shows 1 × 10-2 for All Patients, 0.6 for Early, 9 × 10-4 for Late, and 1 × 10-2 for Recurrent; Vs. Early stage samples yields 5 × 10-4 for Late and 1 × 10-2 for Recurrent.
